questions.text: Walk me through a recent feature you built as a Frontend Engineer. What trade-offs did you make? (based on your profile: No resume summary provided. Ask general interview questions....)
How would you debug a production performance issue in a Frontend Engineer role? (based on your profile: No resume summary provided. Ask general interview questions....)
Explain a time you improved reliability or reduced latency. What metrics did you track? (based on your profile: No resume summary provided. Ask general interview questions....)
Tell me about a conflict in a team and how you resolved it.
Describe a time you received critical feedback. What did you change?
Design a scalable service for scheduling mock interviews with voice recordings and analytics. (Junior)

feedback.summary: Solid structure and relevant examples. Add sharper metrics, clearer trade-offs, and tighter conclusions for each answer.
strengths: Answers are generally structured and on-topic.
You reference implementation details, showing hands-on experience.
Good intent to quantify impact (continue doing this).
improvements: Add concrete metrics (latency, throughput, cost, adoption).
Call out trade-offs explicitly (complexity vs speed vs reliability).
Close each answer with outcomes and learnings.

communication.notes: Good pacing and clarity. Reduce filler and add signposting.
communication.structure: Use STAR for behavioral answers and 'problem → approach → result' for technical answers.

confidence.notes: Tone is professional. Be more decisive when recommending approaches.
confidence.presence: Lead with the strongest point, then support it with evidence.

technical.notes: Shows baseline competency. Go deeper into edge-cases and failure modes.
technical.accuracy: When unsure, state assumptions and validate them.

nextSteps: Run 2 more sessions and focus on quantifying impact.
Practice 60–90s concise answers for top 10 questions.
Add one system design deep-dive per week.
